message:
fix(ssh,termwiz,core): eliminate panics in SFTP, widget, and scan pipeline

1. ssh/sftp/file.rs: 10 bare .unwrap() calls on self.tx (Option)
   replaced with proper error returns. If an SFTP file handle is
   used after close or before initialization, returns an error
   instead of panicking. Added sender() helper for SftpChannelResult
   paths and match-based guard for AsyncRead/AsyncWrite Poll paths.

2. ssh/sftp/dir.rs: Same pattern — .unwrap() on self.tx replaced
   with .ok_or_else() error.

3. termwiz/widgets/mod.rs:621: self.graph.root.unwrap() panics if
   compute_layout is called with no widgets. Changed to .ok_or_else()
   with descriptive error message.

4. scan_pipeline.rs:365: matched.offset + matched.length uses
   unchecked addition. Changed to saturating_add for robustness.

Validated: 267 termwiz, 177 ssh, 21268 core tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -60,7 +60,7 @@ impl Dir {
         let (reply, rx) = bounded(1);
         self.tx
             .as_ref()
-            .unwrap()
+            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("SFTP dir handle not initialized or already closed"))?
             .send(SessionRequest::Sftp(SftpRequest::Dir(DirRequest::ReadDir(
                 self.dir_id,
                 reply,
